title: Critic verdict (bare/headless isolation level)
description: Machine-checkable version of the verdict JSON contract carried as a comment block in templates/prompts/critic-review.md (lines ~154-162) and described in prose in harness/review-protocol.md §2.4 (findings format) / §2.3 (--bare isolation level). Field-identical to that contract by construction. Consumed by plugins/pipeline-core/scripts/critic-bare.mjs, which validates a critic run's final verdict text against this file with a hand-rolled validator (no JSON-Schema library dependency) that understands exactly the keywords used below: type, required, properties, items, enum, additionalProperties. Do not add $ref/oneOf/allOf/pattern/format/etc. to this file without extending that validator in lockstep -- it will silently ignore keywords it does not implement.

findings.description: Ordered by severity (review-protocol §2.4). An empty array is a valid and desired result -- 'no findings' is welcome, not suspicious (anti-overreporting, §2.5).

gap.description: What is missing or deviates vs. spec/guardrail (1-2 sentences).

risk.description: Consequence of the gap. Severity itself is the separate 'severity' field.

evidence.description: file:line, diff hunk, or artifact quote. Claims without a citation are inadmissible (review-protocol §2.5).

spec_ref.description: EARS criterion id or guardrail rule id.

deliberately_not_flagged.description: Canonical rubric (review-protocol §2.4, DoD condition A10): aspects explicitly examined and found ok -- distinguishes 'checked, ok' from 'not looked at'.

trajectory_verdict.description: Were the claimed checks actually executed, per the evidence artifact? Mandatory verdict (review-protocol §2.4).

trajectory_evidence.description: What was checked / what is missing, backing the trajectory_verdict.

briefing_violations.description: Contaminating input the critic was handed despite the input-construction rule (review-protocol §2.2), or an empty array for none.

pass.description: Binary pass/fail. Only requested because the --bare/headless JSON schema always requests one (review-protocol §2.4: 'No overall score' otherwise).
